explicit_tdd_waiver.definition: Allowed only when the accepted BDD contract is mechanics-only, non-code, or otherwise not representable as an automated or manual verification contract.

no_actionable_findings.definition: All required review artifacts exist and contain no critical, high, or explicitly actionable findings.

no_deployment_surface.definition: The accepted change has no deployable, published, environment, or release surface.

no_durable_memory_signal.definition: The run produced no reusable business rule, decision, environment, procedure, deployment, glossary, agent, or user-preference update.

no_external_unknowns.definition: Planning does not depend on current external facts or unknown third-party behavior.

not_homolog_or_no_ui_flow.definition: The target is not staging/homolog or the change has no UI/user-flow surface requiring browser evidence.

no_internal_context.definition: The research question has no relevant repository, memory, code graph, decision, or prior-run context.

no_external_research_needed.definition: The answer can be produced from project-local evidence and does not depend on current external facts.

stepResultSchema.verdictRule: Only PASS satisfies a normal gate. FAIL, BLOCKED, and BLOCKED_TO_PLANNING never pass. SKIPPED passes only when the step declares allow_skip: true.
stepResultSchema.evidenceRule: A PASS result needs at least one evidence item with rung ran or verified unless the gate explicitly allows written evidence.
stepResultSchema.blockerQuestionRule: When a step returns a blocker, fails a gate, or reaches a BLOCKED/FAIL stop, it may include blocker_question: { question: string, options: 1-3 distinct short strings ordered best-first, allow_free_text: true }. The runtime persists these fields under awaiting_user_input and supplies a default options-bearing blocker_question instead of leaving a blocked run with awaiting_user_input:null.
stepResultSchema.policyRule: Workflow policy gates need policy_decision. HUMAN_REVIEW_REQUIRED and BLOCK stop execution at the workflow boundary. Parent-session permission profiles and tool-call approval gates are intentionally absent.

runState.runIdRule: Runtime allocates a unique run_id before workflow start. Templates must not default to active for write paths.
runState.contractPathRule: contract_path must derive from run_id unless the user explicitly imports an external contract.
runState.userInputPathRule: When a blocked run is awaiting_user_input and the interactive picker records an answer, the runtime appends it to .aipi/runtime/runs/{{ run_id }}/USER-INPUT.jsonl before re-executing the blocked step. Headless free text is not silently consumed; the textual prompt is shown and the message continues to the model. Runtime blocks must not dead-end with awaiting_user_input:null.

contextMaterialization.contextFromRule: context_from references step ids. Runtime injects each referenced step's structured result summary first, then bounded artifact excerpts selected by context-curator.
contextMaterialization.missingArtifactRule: A declared produces path missing at step end forces FAIL unless the step result marks that artifact as skipped with an allowed skip condition.
contextMaterialization.tokenBudgetRule: Each agent class may set a budget; context-curator prunes by BDD rule relevance, owned files, recent failures, and evidence rung.
contextMaterialization.userInputRule: Context packets include recent run USER-INPUT.jsonl records so business-rule blocker answers are available when the blocked step resumes.

modelRouting.capabilityFloorRule: Every model class in .aipi/model-classes.yaml declares the floor it needs. Before beta/adversarial-review readiness, .aipi/model-capabilities.json must map each class to a configured provider/model and provide capability evidence for that model. Missing mappings, missing model capability entries, or unmet floors are local blockers, not external evidence gaps.
modelRouting.hostFallbackRule: For local interactive worker execution, an unbound model_class falls back by default to the current supported host session model captured by lifecycle model_select/session_start/before_agent_start into SubagentCoordinator.setHostModel, then passes that real model into createAgentSession. Capture reads preserved user selections first, then routing.model, then defensive event/ctx shapes including event.model.id, ctx.model, and ctx.getModel(). Set allow_fallback:false to fail loud instead. Provenance must record model_resolved as the real host model id, not the host-default sentinel. Non-Anthropic models may be configured for reviewer/worker classes, but the interactive host/orchestrator turn must fail gracefully until that provider is supported by the host adapter.

codeGraph.rule: The code graph is rebuildable cache, not authority. AIPI writes a JSON manifest plus a node:sqlite sidecar for files, symbols, searchable code lines, relationship edges, run outcome summaries, and sqlite-vec symbol/window chunk vectors when the platform extension loads. When sqlite-vec and local Ollama are available, vectors use 1024-dimensional bge-m3 embeddings by default, with dimensions read from semantic-memory.json or AIPI_OLLAMA_DIMENSIONS for future model swaps; code_lines stay line-level for lexical fallback while vector_chunks record path/start_line/end_line metadata. Semantic-only search fails loudly if Ollama or bge-m3 is unavailable, while aipi_callers and aipi_impact continue through SQLite lexical or file lexical fallback; aipi_retrieve fuses semantic chunks, lexical lines, graph proximity, and business-rule/BDD/test relationships with RRF provenance. Onboarding and graph rebuild detect Ollama-up-but-model-missing through /api/tags and surface 'semantic memory is OFF - run `ollama pull bge-m3`, then re-run onboarding / rebuild' instead of silently reporting sqlite+lexical only. A graph built with an old embedding dimension or old line-vector granularity is stale and rebuilt with a matching vec0 table. Rebuildable domain edges connect accepted business rules, BDD contracts, deployment memory, run artifacts, and source files so impact tools can cite business-rule and deployment context. Historical run artifacts distinguish PASS, FAIL, BLOCKED, and SKIPPED outcomes when linking runs back to business rules and impacted code. Domain edge inference canonicalizes deterministic PT/EN aliases and explicit source path references before falling back to token overlap, and records conservative implicit preserve-vs-replace, allow-vs-deny, required-vs-optional, automatic-vs-manual, numeric-mismatch, monetary-mismatch, threshold-direction-mismatch, or sequence-mismatch business-rule conflicts when related rules share enough canonical domain terms. If SQLite or sqlite-vec is unavailable, callers/impact/retrieve tools fall back to SQLite lexical or file lexical scans plus manifest relationships. Markdown remains authoritative over generated index data.

enforcementLevels.prompt_only: Instruction exists, but no runtime block exists.
enforcementLevels.runtime_gate: Pi extension can block, mutate, branch, or require approval.
enforcementLevels.tool_enforced: A tool_call hook or tool override blocks the unsafe action inside the current Pi process before that tool call executes. This is not a security boundary.
enforcementLevels.externally_contained: Execution is isolated by a container, VM, micro-VM, remote sandbox, least-privilege credentials, or equivalent boundary outside the Pi process.

runtimeFit.subagentRule: Pi has no native subagent lifecycle. AIPI workers run through the single AIPI-owned forked pi-subagents runtime behind the stable aipi_* tool surface.
runtimeFit.skillRule: Read-only, single-shot, or lifecycle discipline roles should prefer native Pi Skills before becoming spawned sessions.
runtimeFit.toolScopeRule: pi.setActiveTools is session-wide. Use it at spawned-session setup or restore carefully; use tool_call for per-call stage policy.
runtimeFit.mutationQueueRule: withFileMutationQueue protects mutations inside one runtime. Cross-session/process safety requires orchestrator-owned file allocation, worker-local ownership checks through wrapped write tools, and a shared lock registry.
runtimeFit.communityBackendRule: Community swarm packages may back session-agent lifecycle or messaging, but AIPI owns BDD gates, policy decisions, memory promotion, and workflow state.

subagentBackendOptions.budgetRule: Worker descriptors may set budget.timeout_ms, timeoutMs, max_runtime_ms, maxRuntimeMs, max_tool_calls, or maxToolCalls. The coordinator aborts the forked runtime on timeout with abort_reason=budget_timeout and fails a completed worker whose reported tool_call_count exceeds max_tool_calls with abort_reason=budget_max_tool_calls. State, counters, and aipi.subagents.event traces are persisted.
subagentBackendOptions.toolTraceRule: The forked runtime reports aggregate tool_call_count and AIPI records it on status, step_result provenance, and lifecycle traces without persisting tool inputs or content.
subagentBackendOptions.lifecycleTraceRule: Workers emit queued, started, aipi_forked_subagent_start, worker_prompt_start, worker_prompt_end, aipi_forked_subagent_end, done/failed/cancelled, and worker_cleanup events with elapsed_ms, prompt_elapsed_ms, budget, model provenance, runtime_root, and tool-call counters where available.
subagentBackendOptions.workerProviderRule: Forked workers run through the host Pi runtime with the AIPI provider extension loaded by the wrapper. The coordinator passes the captured or configured provider/model into the forked runtime, strips fallbackModels, and limits availableModels to the selected worker model when it is provider-qualified. Child sessions receive read/grep/find/ls as built-ins and AIPI's guarded write extension, not the unguarded builtin write.
subagentBackendOptions.hostModelFallbackRule: When a spawn descriptor has an unresolved or unknown model_class and fallback is allowed, AIPI prefers the model captured in SubagentCoordinator.getHostModel from lifecycle model_select/session_start/before_agent_start, then falls back to tool-call ctx.model fields. aipi_agent_status and step_result strict provenance show model_requested, model_resolved as the real host model id actually passed to the worker, model_fallback=true, and a warning such as running on host model <id> (class <name> unbound). allow_fallback:false rejects the spawn before owned-file allocation. Worker fallback remains provider-agnostic for provider-qualified models such as Anthropic, OpenAI, Codex/OpenAI-Codex, or another configured provider. The interactive host/orchestrator turn is narrower: unsupported non-Anthropic host providers must be reported gracefully instead of crashing.
subagentBackendOptions.piSubagentsPhaseOneRule: pi-subagents source is forked from pi-subagents@0.28.0 under extensions/aipi/runtime/vendor/pi-subagents with LICENSE and VENDOR.md. The npm package pi-subagents is not a dependency or optionalDependency, there is no separately-loaded pi-subagents extension, and no backend-selection environment flag exists. AIPI owns runtime/pi-subagents.js, calls the vendored runSync entrypoint through jiti, writes agent/sessions/artifacts/results under .aipi/runtime/subagents, disables fallbackModels, passes AIPI_SUBAGENTS_PROJECT_ROOT/AIPI_SUBAGENTS_AGENT_ID/AIPI_SUBAGENTS_OWNED_FILES into the child, loads extensions/aipi/runtime/aipi-guarded-write-child.js as the worker write tool, and exposes the stable aipi_* tool surface by default. Non-auth utility deps kept are jiti@2.7.0 for loading the vendored TS runSync path and typebox@1.2.16 for the vendored structured-output validator; @earendil-works/pi-tui is not a direct AIPI dependency because the TUI extension surface is no longer loaded. Live provider-inheritance proof is manual: start a real AIPI/Pi session via `aipi` with provider auth/model selected, then run `/aipi-pi-subagents-spike`. GO requires assistant_text_ok=true for the child worker's OK reply, provider_event_observed=true from .aipi/runtime/provider-events.jsonl, project-scoped .aipi/runtime/subagents writes, and no provider/model other than the selected worker model is attempted.
subagentBackendOptions.cleanupRule: aipi_cleanup_agents applies retention cleanup only to terminal workers, releases owned-file allocations, emits a cleanup trace, returns aipi.subagents.cleanup.v1, and never deletes durable project/user memory.
subagentBackendOptions.steeringRule: aipi_steer_agent is registered but returns accepted=false with an explicit reason until worker prompt/response boundaries are resumable.
subagentBackendOptions.redispatchRule: Workflow adapters call coordinator.dispatch(descriptor). If a restored interrupted worker matches step_id, catalog agent id, and owned_files, the coordinator marks the old worker redispatched, releases its owned-file allocation, and spawns a fresh worker from the current descriptor/context.
subagentBackendOptions.criterionZero: Probe A's host tool_call hooks observed zero worker calls, but live Round 29 evidence proved the forked pi-subagents runtime can spawn real workers through the host Pi/Anthropic OAuth path. AIPI now wraps that single runtime with owned-file allocation, a guarded-shell worker policy, timeout/tool-count budgets, model provenance, and project-scoped runtime directories. The worker toolset is read/grep/find/ls/write plus aipi_guarded_bash (the watchdog-wrapped, project-root-scoped shell that lets a worker actually run tests/typecheck/build to VERIFY); raw bash/user_bash/exec/edit are absent. Probe A tested host tool_call attribution, a mechanism the design no longer relies on.

aipiToolSurface.rule: Every aipi_* tool referenced by agents/catalog.yaml must be implemented in the AIPI Pi extension or explicitly listed as specificationOnly for a future surface. The validator fails on silent catalog/runtime drift.

commandWatchdog.rule: AIPI shell execution should route through aipi_guarded_bash for agent-run commands. The guard refuses common interactive REPL/editor traps and pathological recursive project-root searches before spawning, including raw grep/find scans that would traverse .aipi/state, .git, or node_modules without real exclude/prune flags, and steers AIPI-internal runtime strings toward extensions/aipi/runtime or aipi_retrieve/aipi_callers instead of full-tree scans. It tracks start time, last stdout/stderr time, total runtime, command, pid, and process tree, kills stuck commands on silence timeout or hard cap, invokes a background stuck-vs-working check only for ambiguous long-running build/test commands, records a diagnostic note, and appends aipi.command-watchdog.v1 traces. The user_bash hook remains observational and must not reintroduce broad input blocking.

lifecycleHooks.providerPayloadPolicy: before_provider_request redacts obvious secret patterns and prunes duplicate/oversized AIPI context messages before returning the replacement payload to Pi.
lifecycleHooks.rule: Lifecycle hooks preserve active run pointers across session transitions, restore the latest aipi.subagents.state snapshot on session_start by marking queued/running workers interrupted, route recognized natural-language input through AIPI workflow commands before the agent turn, but auto-dispatch only when a genuinely executable workflow adapter is available from a coordinator with spawn and collect; otherwise it falls back to a visible workflow suggestion before any run is created. Continuation-of-existing-work requests without an active run do not spawn fresh planning. The local workflow adapter is a non-executable fallback and must never be used by auto-dispatch for agent-backed workflows. Render the blocked-run picker with ctx.ui.select/ctx.ui.input when awaiting_user_input.options exists and ctx.hasUI is true, record picker answers before resuming awaiting_user_input runs, fall back to a visible textual prompt without swallowing headless free text, provide AIPI compaction summaries for active runs, inject and prune compact AIPI context pointers before provider calls, resolve model_select and thinking_level_select from event model_class, event agent_id, or the active workflow step using explicit env overrides, .aipi/model-capabilities.json class mappings, current-session models, and thinking-level policy, record manual model drift and unresolved class-only routes in .aipi/runtime/model-routing.jsonl, apply provider payload redaction/pruning before model calls, run agent_end/turn_end/message_end discipline audits for finish-turn, outcome-first, and claim-evidence with message_end warning on unsupported fixed/passed/safe/done claims that lack a concrete evidence rung while returning undefined or a same-role message per the Pi message_end contract, observe user_bash discipline hooks without blocking user commands through a permission policy, normalize provider token/cost usage when metadata is available, estimate missing cost only from fresh .aipi/provider-pricing.json rates that include source_url and checked_at metadata, report optional provider budget state from .aipi/provider-budget.json, record cost_unknown instead of zero spend when token usage has no provider cost or fresh local rate, and normalize tool/provider evidence without storing provider payloads or secrets. declaredPromptOnlyHooks records discipline hook names that are known from runtime-hooks.md but not implemented in verifiedHooks; they remain prompt_only until the runtime registers them.

isolationModel.failClosedRule: Any worker descriptor requesting an isolation other than pi_subagents is rejected before owned-file allocation instead of silently downgrading. Workers always run with cwd set to the project root and runtime paths under .aipi/runtime/subagents.
isolationModel.rule: AIPI has one worker runtime: the AIPI-owned fork of pi-subagents. It is not an npm dependency, not loaded as a separate Pi extension, and not selected by an environment flag. AIPI supervises spawn/status/collect/cancel/cleanup/steer, owned-file allocation, timeout/tool-count budgets, model provenance, a guarded-shell policy (workers get aipi_guarded_bash; raw bash/user_bash denied), and project-scoped runtime directories around the fork.

singleWorktreeSessionAgents.shellRule: SINGLE-LEAD (non-fanout) forked pi_subagents workers receive aipi_guarded_bash — the AIPI command-watchdog shell (refuses interactive REPL/editor traps, kills stuck/silent commands, cwd confined fail-closed to the project root) — so they can run tests/typecheck/build/lint/git to actually VERIFY a change instead of only documenting it. PARALLEL fanout (review_swarm) workers receive NO shell (allow_shell:false): a shell bypasses the owned-file/controller-path write guards, so granting it concurrently would void write-disjointness and .git/.aipi/memory protection — those parallel workers review by reading the code and the verify step's evidence. Raw bash/user_bash/exec are never given to any worker (they remain in denyTools). A single-lead worker runs alone (same trust level as the main flexible agent), so its guarded shell is acceptable.
singleWorktreeSessionAgents.writeScopeRule: Each worker's write tool is scoped by step. Analysis/review/planning steps are artifacts-only: they may write only their declared owned files. Code-writing steps (stage implementation, fix, or tdd, or an explicit write_scope: project) get a project write scope so the worker can create or edit the actual source/test files its change requires, since a root-cause fix cannot pre-declare every file it touches. Controller-owned state (.aipi/memory, .aipi/runtime/runs non-artifact paths, .git) stays blocked under every scope. The scope is carried on the spawn descriptor write_scope, enforced host-side by OwnedFileRegistry.grantProjectScope/owns and worker-side by AIPI_SUBAGENTS_WRITE_SCOPE in aipi-guarded-write-child. Project write scope is granted only to single-lead code-writing steps that run sequentially; parallel review fanout stays artifact-disjoint.

adapterTransitiveBehavior.identityBlock: The upstream adapter removes any system block equal to the Claude Code identity text before Anthropic OAuth requests.
adapterTransitiveBehavior.billingHeader: The upstream adapter preserves an existing x-anthropic-billing-header system text block or injects a synthetic one when absent.
adapterTransitiveBehavior.docsContext: The upstream adapter can strip and re-inject Pi docs context around Anthropic OAuth provider requests.
adapterTransitiveBehavior.reviewRule: Before package bumps, review src/claude-oauth-adapter.ts for identity-block, billing-header, docs-context, before_provider_request, and provider hook changes; docs/anthropic-auth-integration.md must disclose these transitive request mutations.

anthropic.rule: AIPI keeps @ersintarhan/pi-toolkit pinned as the upstream source of the Anthropic OAuth adapter, but does not autoload the package's broad index.ts by default. The default provider extension is an AIPI-owned wrapper that imports only src/claude-oauth-adapter.ts. The wrapper intentionally inherits the adapter's disclosed Claude Code identity-block handling, x-anthropic-billing-header injection/preservation, Pi docs context handling, and provider hook mutations. AIPI still owns model-class routing, workflow gates, memory, and policy.
